title: NOC 22312 – Industrial Instrument Technicians and Mechanics | GTR Immigration
description: Industrial Instrument Technicians and Mechanics are in strong demand across Canada, especially in heavy-industry and energy sectors where automated control systems are critical to safe and efficient operations. Classified under NOC 22312 (TEER 2), this trade-technical occupation is well aligned with:

classification.teer: 2 – College diploma or apprenticeship normally required

duties: Test flow, pressure, level & temperature instruments
Diagnose faults using pneumatic, electrical & electronic devices
Repair or replace sensors, transmitters & PLC systems
Install new control systems in industrial plants
Advise plant operators on system performance
employment_requirements: Either:4–5-year apprenticeship in industrial instrumentation, or2-year college program plus work experience
Red Seal endorsement for national mobility
job_outlook: Demand remains steady, especially for candidates with:
additional_information: Canadian employers in power generation, petrochemical processing, mining, pulp & paper, and manufacturing continue to recruit instrumentation specialists to operate and maintain complex process-control systems.
Typical annual earnings range from $44,300–$111,600 CAD, depending on certification, province, and industry.

Skilled-trade PR eligibilityRed Seal portability across provincesStrong wages in industrial regionsUnionized roles in utilities & energy plantsPublic healthcare accessAdvancement to senior technician or supervisory roles
